mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (0.13% as of June 2015) keeps the equity risk premium compressed by lowering the discount rate applied to future cash flows, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window while FEDFUNDS stays under 0.5%, or mean forward return in low-rate periods is not significantly greater than the full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below 5.5% - approaching Fed estimates of full employment - markets begin pricing an accelerated liftoff timeline, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as rate-hike expectations get pulled forward and discount rates rise.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE is below 5.5%, forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the mean triggered return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS month-over-month increase exceeding 200k) occurring simultaneously with a still-near-zero FEDFUNDS rate creates a policy-divergence signal that markets interpret as an imminent tightening surprise, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days; the effect requires both conditions jointly, since strong payrolls alone in a high-rate regime would not carry the same surprise magnitude.
failure_condition: The joint-trigger subsample shows forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from or greater than unconditional baseline returns (p > 0.05), or single-series PAYEMS-only models achieve equal or higher Rank IC than the interaction model.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) during a post-crisis recovery regime signal durable improvement in household demand and construction-sector employment, translating into positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations for cyclical sectors improve; this relationship is expected to weaken or reverse once the housing cycle matures into late-cycle overbuilding.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST 3-month trend and forward 60d return is below 0.03 during 2012-2015 recovery window, or positive HOUST trend periods do not show significantly higher 60d returns than negative-trend periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO of more than 1% over three months, driven by the 2014-2015 oil price collapse and dollar strength suppressing manufacturing output, signals deteriorating corporate earnings for industrial and energy sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days once the decline threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns following an INDPRO 3-month decline exceeding 1% are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the triggered-sample mean return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer spending, which is the largest component of GDP; positive RSAFS momentum is expected to produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as it confirms near-term earnings support for consumer-facing sectors.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 5d return is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive RSAFS growth months do not show significantly higher forward returns than negative growth months (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Low and stable CPI inflation combined with near-zero FEDFUNDS implies a persistently low real interest rate environment, delaying the perceived need for aggressive tightening and supporting equity valuations; the joint condition of CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth below 1% and FEDFUNDS below 0.5% is expected to produce positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon, an effect not captured by either series independently since low inflation alone without accommodative rates would not sustain the valuation support.
failure_condition: During periods satisfying both conditions jointly, forward 60d returns are not significantly higher than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or a single-series FEDFUNDS-only model achieves equal or higher Rank IC than the joint interaction model.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor market tightening regime, where UNRATE has fallen steadily for multiple years toward structural lows, further declines in UNRATE lose their historically positive equity signal and instead precede negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days because markets begin discounting wage-inflation-driven tightening risk rather than celebrating labor market strength.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 5.5% and declining for 6+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=5.3 as of 2015-06-01, down from 6.1 a year earlier
failure_condition: In late-cycle low-UNRATE regimes, forward 60d returns following continued UNRATE declines remain positive in more than 55% of cases, contradicting the hypothesized sign flip relative to early/mid-cycle regimes.
